title: Tags, Versions, Releases, and Provenance · Repository Checkpoint
summary: Prepare a release candidate whose source identity, version, evidence, and published reference can be independently verified. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a signed-off release evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.
Create, inspect, verify, delete, and cautiously replace tags while recognizing immutable-release expectations.
Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Lightweight Annotated Tags · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Release v4.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

Which action demonstrates Lightweight Annotated Tags without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Lightweight Annotated Tags evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Lightweight Annotated Tags assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Signature Trust: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Release v6.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits. Competency probe: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.
steps.why: Retrieving Signature Trust through the concrete a signed-off release evidence record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.
